title: Extensible API providers (config + plugin)
description:
Allow adding OpenAI-compatible API providers via:
1. Config: Edit config.toml with id, name, base_url, env_var, type
2. Plugin: Drop-in file with pre-configured provider (for non-technical users)

Examples: xAI, Together, Fireworks, self-hosted LLMs.
Plugins are pre-packaged configs that can be shared/distributed.

logs.msg: Potential provider: Modal (https://modal.com/blog/try-glm-5) — OpenAI-compatible inference endpoint, worth evaluating as a provider adapter

Rescoped: NOT a plugin system. Simple TOML config for OpenAI-compatible providers: name + base_url + api_key_env. Covers xAI, Mistral, Together AI, Perplexity, local vLLM/llama.cpp, any future OpenAI-compat endpoint. We already have the OpenAI client — parameterize base URL. ~50 lines. Cut the plugin/extension part; keep the config part.

Scope updated: (1) OpenAI-compat base_url config for xAI/Mistral/Together/Fireworks/Perplexity (~50 lines, do first). (2) Evaluate llm crate (graniet/llm v1.3.7) for replacing openai_compat/ layer and adding Cohere/HuggingFace/Bedrock — prior research rated it best fit (text+tools, streaming, thinking, cache, feature-flags, maps to StreamEvent). Risk: single maintainer, edition 2021. (3) Add common providers natively: DeepSeek already has quirks so needs own path; Mistral near OpenAI-compat; Cohere/Bedrock need new protocol code or llm crate. See ai/research/provider-crates-2026-02.md and ai/research/rust-llm-crates-survey-2026-02.md.
